calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-25-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Triay for and with Allen Drucker (Former Trustee).

The Court noted that there was no appearance by Illona Barbaria, and expressed concern about lack of proof of service of the order granting the motion to relieve her Counsel and Ms. Barbaria not attending previous hearings. Mr. Triay stated that service of the fee petition was made at her home, that they were told that she was in Hawaii and that they had no contact with Ms. Barbaria. The sale was confirmed at $ based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The accounting petition and the fee petition were taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Award Of Attorneys Fees To Attorney For Former Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-25-2011 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Award Of Attorneys Fees To Attorney For Former Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-14-2011 Continued To Feb-17-11 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Feb-07-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Rothman for Illona Barbaria.

Mr. Rothman stated that he has not heard from Ms. Barbaria. The Court granted the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-15-2010 Continued To Feb-17-11 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Triay for and with Allen Drucker (Former Trustee). Robert Rothman for Illona Barbaria.

Mr. Rothman requested a continuance to file a Motion to Withdraw as Counsel and stated that his Client is now a missing person. Mr. Triay stated that he would like to have a trial set, that he would like to have someone appointed to look after the asset and that the $20,000.00 set aside was never returned. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel is to be set with a hearing date of January 26, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting petition was set for hearing on Febraury 17, 2011 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-01-2010 Continued To Dec-15-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Rothman for Ilona Barbaria (Successor Trustee). Charles Triay for and with Allen Drucker (Former Trustee).

Mr. Rothman stated that they mediated, that the matter was not resolved, and that he was not able to speak with his Client as she was back East visiting with a family member. Mr. Rothman also requested a continuance stating that there was an issue of further representation of his Client. Mr. Triay requested the matter be set for hearing. The matter was continued to December 15, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of setting.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-13-2010 Continued To Dec-01-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-28-2010 Continued To Oct-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

HEARING ORDERED FOR APPLICATION OF WAIVER OF COURT FEES
Off Calendar Dec-21-2010 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-27-2010 Continued To Sep-28-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Triay for and with Allen Drucker (Former Trustee). Robert Rothman for Ilona Barbaria.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than September 24, 2010. A status conference is set for September 28, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court granted the application for waiver of fees subject to recoupment down the line. Mr. Rothman stated that the $20,000.00 that was to be returned to Mr. Triay's Client was spent to stop foreclosure on the property, and that if they were successful in stopping the foreclosure and selling the property, that the money would go back to the Trust. The accounting petition was also continued to September 28, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-09-2010 Continued To Jul-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

HEARING ORDERED FOR APPLICATION OF WAIVER OF COURT FEES
Off Calendar Jun-22-2010 Continued To Jul-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Triay for and with Allen Drucker. Robert Rothman for and with Illona Barbaria.

Mr. Rothman stated that he and his Client went to Union Bank, that there was a balance of $23,785.22. that the Wells Fargo Bank account had been closed, that the Bank had concern with the checks being in the name of Trust Management Services, that they could not determine if there is more money in another account and that his Client was concerned about the $20,000.00 withhold amount disappearing. Mr. Triay stated that a box of all of the records was delivered to Mr. Rothman's office on May 21, 2010 and that the $20,000.00 should be returned. The Court stated that the order is to remain in place, that the $20,000.00 is to go into Mr. Triay's Client Trust account and that it is to remain under Court jurisdiction.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of The Former Trustee'S Accounting And Turning Over Of Assets
Off Calendar Jun-22-2010 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of The Former Trustee'S Accounting And Turning Over Of Assets
Off Calendar Jun-09-2010 Continued To Jun-22-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

HEARING ORDERED FOR APPLICATION OF WAIVER OF COURT FEES
Off Calendar Jun-09-2010 Continued To Jun-22-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Rothman for and with Illona Barbaria. Charles Triay for and with Allen Drucker.

Counsel appeared and stated that the documents and the assets were turned over, that the Successor Trustee was required by the Bank to take over the existing account, and that there was an issue with the $20,000.00 withhold amount due to the inability to verify if that amount was withdrawn by Mr. Drucker or if it is still in the account now in possession of Ms. Barbaria. Mr. Triay stated that his Client did not have the funds, that they did everything they had asked and that opposing Counsel was now imposing new conditions. The matter was continued to June 22, 2010 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow both Counsel to obtain closing statements and opening statements for clarification of the balances.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-20-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Rothman for Illona Barbaria. Charles Triay for Allen Drucker

Mr. Rothman stated that Mr. Drucker signed the stipulation and resignation, that he presented a faxed copy to Ms. Versoza (Probate Examiner) and provided a copy to the Court. In addition, he stated that opposing Counsel stated that he would file the original stipulation when he received it back from his Client or file a faxed copy of it. The Court stated that it would like to see the original signature and the original stipulation/resignation filed. Mr. Rothman stated that the urgency was with Wells Fargo Bank and the property going into foreclosure, the possible need to sell the property, his Client not having authority and that the Trust was running on a negative of over $2,000.00 a month. Mr. Traiy arrived late with the original stipulation which was filed by the Clerk. The Court granted the Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee (signing the order for the same in open court). Mr. Drcuker is to file an accounting and turn over assets within 45 days. Status of filing of Mr. Drucker's account and turning over of the assets, files and documents, and status of the Request for Fee Waiver was set for June 9, 2010 at 1:30 p.m., in Dept. 204. If Mr. Drucker does not comply with the above by that date, the Court will issue an Order to Show Cause as to why Mr. Drucker should not appear and why sanctions should not be imposed for failure to comply with the Court order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

HEARING ORDERED FOR APPLICATION OF WAIVER OF COURT FEES
Off Calendar May-20-2010 Continued To Jun-09-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-12-2010 Continued To May-20-10 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Rothman for and with Illona Barbaria. Charles Triay for Mark Drucker (Current Trustee).

Mr. Rothman informed the Court that they had reached a stipulation, stated that Mr. Drucker agreed to resign in Ms. Barbaria's favor and that he agreed to file an accounting within 45 days which will cover his tenure from January 7, 2009 through yesterday. In addition, he stated that they agreed to a $20,000.00 withholding pending submission of the accounting to determine whether there is a need for litigation. Mr. Triay stated that his Client was out of town, that he would be back on Monday and that his Client had not signed the resignation. Mr. Rothman also stated that Mark's interest was settled out, that his Client was the sole Beneficiary, that there was a need to sell the property and that there was an issue with Wells Fargo Bank. The Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee was continued to May 20, 2010 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204. If the resignation is signed and filed prior to the hearing date, the matter may be pre-granted and no appearance will be required. Status of filing of the accounting will be set 45 days out. The Application of Waiver of Fees was not addressed. Therefore, that matter will also be continued to May 20, 2010 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
